page: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The server is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 1843 GB of local NVMe storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with large-block uncached read bandwidth reaching 260.21 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 127.25 GB/sec. The system demonstrates robust capabilities in database operations, scoring 22997.62 on PassMark, and achieves a high static web serving throughput of 48.57 GB/sec. CPU-bound tasks show average to strong performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 50651.26 and a multi-core stress-ng result of 46635.68. However, the lack of dedicated GPU hardware limits its utility for heavy machine learning tasks; while small and medium LLM inference speeds remain average, text generation on a large 70B model drops to a poor 2.33 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E48ds_v5 offers high resource density, combining substantial memory capacity with a large local disk. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large relational databases, enterprise applications, and data caching layers like Redis, where it achieves 46.94 million SET operations per second. It is also well-suited for static web serving and data processing tasks that benefit from fast local storage and high memory bandwidth, though it is not recommended for large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this x86_64 server includes 1843 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks reveal strong memory bandwidth, with large-block uncached reads at 260.21 GB/sec, and excellent database performance, scoring 22997.62 in PassMark database operations. While CPU performance is robust, the absence of a GPU results in poor text generation speeds for large 70B LLM models. This server is highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v5 is an Intel-powered, memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-capacity local storage and strong memory bandwidth for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores
384.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
1843 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput database performance
Poorly suited for large-scale LLM text generation due to lack of GPU
Optimized for memory-intensive enterprise applications and database hosting
